cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user is asking which statement reflects the writer's opinion of the story. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the story. The main character is a guy who has cancer and lives at home with his mother. He decides to go out and visits a music store daily, buying CDs. The girl there wraps them and he keeps them unopened. Eventually, he leaves his phone number but dies before she can call him. His mother finds out all the CDs had a note from Sophie asking him out. The moral is about not waiting to express feelings because life is short. The question is about the writer's opinion. The options are: A. Don't wait to show your feelings. B. Life always goes like that. C. There is only one chance for everyone. D. Show your feelings in a proper way. Looking at the story's ending, the message is clear: don't wait to express how you feel because you might not get another chance. The story's title or the closing line says, "That's the way life is. Don't wait to show those special people the way you feel. Tomorrow would be too late." Option A directly aligns with that message. Option B is a bit vague.
